title: Radiant Spanish Chicken Cocido Madrileno
description: A citrusy, crisp Spanish chicken stew infused with malt vinegar and a subtle tadka, perfect for a romantic dinner setting.
instructions: Season chicken thighs with smoked paprika, ground cumin, ground ginger, salt, and pepper.
Sear the chicken in a large pot with a tablespoon of olive oil until golden brown on both sides, then remove.
Add minced garlic, sliced carrots, quartered onion, and bell pepper to the pot, sautéing until vegetables soften, about 5 minutes.
Return the chicken to the pot and pour in chicken broth, malt vinegar, lime juice, and bay leaf, bringing to a simmer.
Cover and cook on low heat for 45 minutes until chicken is tender and the broth has slightly thickened.
Remove the bay leaf, discard it, and let the stew rest for 5 minutes.
Ladle the cocido into bowls, ensuring each serving receives a generous amount of chicken, vegetables, and broth.
Garnish with freshly chopped cilantro and a drizzle of olive oil if desired.
